title: 5 Tequila Cocktails At Home
full_text: 5 Easy Tequila Cocktails You Can Make At Home - Palate Asia Main Eat Drink Reviews Insider At Home About Us Download Our Magazine Q2 2026 Q1 2026 Vol 1 Palate Dining Guide 2025-2026 Main Eat Drink Reviews Insider At Home About Us Download Our Magazine Q2 2026 Q1 2026 Vol 1 Palate Dining Guide 2025-2026 Now Reading: 5 Easy Tequila Cocktails You Can Make At Home 1 01 5 Easy Tequila Cocktails You Can Make At Home Main // Eat // Drink // Reviews // Insider // At Home // About Us // Download Our Magazine // Q2 2026 // Q1 2026 // Vol 1 Palate Dining Guide 2025-2026 // Home Drink 5 Easy Tequila Cocktails You Can Make At Home 5 Easy Tequila Cocktails You Can Make At Home Colin Gomez At Home ,  Drink 1 month ago Share If your tequila experience begins and ends with salt, lime, and emotional damage, it might be time to branch out. These easy cocktails are a much better use for the bottle in your kitchen. The night is slipping away. You’ve got the vaguest sense that it could still be something more, but how? You scan the kitchen and spot your half-empty bottle of tequila and a few leftover limes in the fridge. What could you do with that? Turns out, quite a lot actually. Tequila is a spirit that’s far more versatile than it gets credit for. It can be bright and refreshing or smoky and serious depending on how you approach it. It’s far more refined than those shudder-inducing nights from your uni days would suggest, and in this article, I’m going to show you how to make the most of it. As usual, we won’t be needing an apron, a shaker set, or any of that jazz. We’re keeping things easy, unfussy, and firmly within the limits of what can be achieved while mildly tipsy in your own kitchen. So without further ado, let’s make something worth drinking. Photography: @highwaterlondon | Instagram 1. Margarita Of course we had to begin with the Margarita. It is the quintessential tequila cocktail after all, and basically what the G&T is to gin. Whether you’re at a beach bar or partying it up at the club, the Margarita is a drink at home almost anywhere—and it can be at your home too. Sharp, citrusy, salty, you know the drill. It’s definitive proof that limes and tequila were made for each other. Ingredients: 50ml tequila 25ml lime juice 20ml triple sec (or Cointreau) Ice Optional: salt rim, lime wheel Method: Run a lime wedge around the rim of your glass and dip it in salt if you want the full experience. Add tequila, lime juice, and triple sec to a glass with ice. Stir well until chilled. Garnish with lime if you’re feeling theatrical. It’s the classic and the go-to for a reason. I don’t need to tell you much more that you don’t already know. And the best part about making it at home is that you don’t need to keep count of how many you’ve had or worry about a repeat of “the incident”… you know the one. 2. Paloma Photography: Esmihel Muhammed | Pexels The Margarita may be the life of the party, but the Paloma is the guy everybody chats with at the snack bowl and goes, “hey, you know what, this guy’s actually pretty cool.” It’s light and fizzy, with a citrus-forward zing that makes it oh-so refreshing, not to mention dangerously drinkable. And in true chill-guy fashion, it’s also easy and unfussy to make. Ingredients: 50ml tequila 100ml grapefruit soda 10ml lime juice Ice Optional: salt rim or grapefruit wedge Method: Fill a tall glass with ice. Pour in tequila and lime juice. Top with grapefruit soda. Give it a gentle stir. Garnish if you’d like to pretend you planned ahead. Bubbly and effervescent with just the right amount of bitterness to round things off, the Paloma makes for a quick and easy mix-up on a sweltering afternoon, or something for guests to sip on at a barbecue. 3. Tequila Sunrise Photography: Shameel mukkath | Pexels I love the Tequila Sunrise for how retro it looks and feels. For some reason, it conjures up images of synth keyboards, analog glitches, Ferrari Testarossas, and Miami skylines at dusk (I know it’s called Sunrise, but whatever). For how extra it looks, it’s actually laughably easy to make. Ingredients: 50ml tequila 100ml orange juice 10ml grenadine Ice Optional: orange slice or cherry Method: Fill a glass with ice. Pour in tequila and orange juice. Slowly pour grenadine down the side of the glass. Watch it sink and do the whole “sunrise” thing. Garnish if you’re committed to the aesthetic. Sweet and smooth—if you distilled an 80s summer playlist into a cocktail, this is probably what you’d get. Sip on it and be swept away to a land where neon lights reflect on wet pavement, palm trees sway against a purple-orange sky, and somewhere, a lonely saxophone solo plays for a gritty hardboiled neo-noir detective. 4. Ranch Water Photography: Felix Rottmann | Pexels This is one drink that is, I think we can all agree, thankfully not self-descriptive. Despite its name, it’s actually one of tequila’s greatest low-effort triumphs. Originating from Texas, it’s a crisp, clean, no-nonsense cocktail that’s a
